answer:
We are waiting for the next occurrence of the value \(X_0\) after time 0. Since the draws are i.i.d., the waiting time follows a geometric distribution with success probability \(p = P(X = X_0)\). The expected waiting time is \(1/p\), and because \(X_0\) is random, we average over its distribution, giving \(E(N) = E[1/p(X_0)]\). For a uniform distribution over 100 values, \(p(x) = 1/100\) for each \(x\), so \(E(N) = 100\).

\boxed{H}
